prompt: Write a Python script that: (1) downloads all HIV-1 protease sequences from the Los Alamos HIV Sequence Database or NCBI (≥5,000 sequences from clinical isolates), (2) aligns sequences to the HXB2 reference using MAFFT, (3) computes site-wise entropy for each codon position to identify hyper-variable positions, (4) maps IAS-USA major drug resistance mutations (D30N, M46I, I50V, etc.) onto the alignment and reports frequency per mutation per subtype (A, B, C, CRF01_AE), (5) outputs a heatmap of resistance mutation frequency by subtype using seaborn, and a CSV of per-site entropy values. Use Biopython, pandas, seaborn, and include a subtype classification step.
source_citation: Derived from NCBI GenBank and Los Alamos HIV database (2024_10); target: HIV-1 protease; organism: HIV-1; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: retrovirus replication enzyme (drug resistance surveillance target)
